old: Please keep this for me for safekeeping.
new: Please hold onto this for me.
reason: Redundant phrasing: 'keep' and 'for safekeeping' say the same thing.

old: to accomplish
new: to reach
reason: 达成 means 'to reach/arrive at (an agreement, consensus, goal)'. All three sentences show 达成协议/目标/共识 and translations say 'reached', confirming the definition should be 'to reach', not 'to accomplish'.

old: 大多数学生通过了考试。
new: 他们大多是学生。
reason: Headword 大多 is absent; sentence uses 大多数 (a different word). Sentence must contain 大多.

old: Most students passed the exam.
new: Most of them are students.
reason: Updated to match corrected sentence_1.

old: 大多数人喜欢夏天。
new: 这里的人大多喜欢夏天。
reason: Headword 大多 is absent; sentence uses 大多数 instead.

old: Most people like summer.
new: Most people here like summer.
reason: Updated to match corrected sentence_2.

old: 大多数孩子都喜欢玩游戏。
new: 孩子们大多喜欢玩游戏。
reason: Headword 大多 is absent; sentence uses 大多数 instead.

old: Most children like to play games.
new: Most children like playing games.
reason: Updated to match corrected sentence_3.

old: 把这些书单独放在一个架子上。
new: 这些文件单放在一个文件夹里。
reason: Headword 单 is absent; all sentences use the compound 单独 (itself a separate HSK word), not 单 alone.

old: Place these books separately on one shelf.
new: These documents are kept separately in one folder.
reason: Updated to match corrected sentence_1.

old: 他把问题单独列出来了。
new: 请把这道题单列出来。
reason: Headword 单 is absent; uses 单独 instead.

old: He listed the problems separately.
new: Please list this question separately.
reason: Updated to match corrected sentence_2.

old: 请把这件事单独处理。
new: 这件事要单独处理。
reason: Headword 单 is absent; uses 单独 instead.

old: Please handle this matter separately.
new: This matter should be handled separately.
reason: Updated to match corrected sentence_3.

old: I want to talk with you privately.
new: I want to talk with you one-on-one.
reason: 我想单独和你谈谈 means 'talk with you alone/one-on-one', not 'privately' (which implies secrecy).

old: single
new: uniform; monotonous
reason: The sentences illustrate 单一 meaning lack of variety (few product types, monotonous diet, narrow business scope). 'Uniform; monotonous' better captures this usage than 'single'.

old: to register
new: to log in
reason: 登录 means 'to log in', not 'to register' (注册). Row 3284 already has 登录 'to log in'; this row has a wrong definition.

old: 你可以在这里登录注册一个账号。
new: 新用户可以在这里登录系统。
reason: Original sentence conflates 登录 with 注册; must illustrate 登录 = to log in.

old: You can register an account here.
new: New users can log in to the system here.
reason: Updated to match corrected sentence_1.

old: 新用户需要先登录并注册信息。
new: 每次打开网站都需要登录。
reason: Original sentence conflates 登录 with 注册; must illustrate only 登录 = to log in.

old: New users need to log in and register their information first.
new: You need to log in every time you open the website.
reason: Updated to match corrected sentence_2.

old: 我帮她登录注册了一个新账号。
new: 我忘记了登录密码。
reason: Original sentence conflates 登录 with 注册; must illustrate only 登录 = to log in.

old: I helped her register a new account.
new: I forgot my login password.
reason: Updated to match corrected sentence_3.

old: The airplane is waiting on Earth's surface.
new: The airplane is waiting on the ground.
reason: 地面 in the context of an airplane means 'ground/tarmac', not 'Earth's surface'. Translation sounds unnatural.

old: 他们的故事让大家感到动人。
new: 他们的故事非常动人，听了都落泪。
reason: 让大家感到动人 is unnatural Chinese; 动人 is an adjective that does not follow 感到 naturally.

old: Their story made everyone feel touched.
new: Their story was so touching that everyone was moved to tears.
reason: Updated to match corrected sentence_3.
